title: Casearia piparea
taxonomy: Casearia piparea is a species of flowering plant in the family Salicaceae, commonly known as the willow family. It is native to tropical Africa. The plant is classified under the genus Casearia, which comprises about 60 species of flowering plants. The genus Casearia is part of the order Malpighiales, within the clade rosid. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its flowers, leaves, and growth habits.

intro: Casearia selloana, also known as Casearia piparea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Salicaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are useful in treating fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections.
